schedule.title: SNC1W Semester 2 (2025-2026)
schedule.overview: Based on the course calendar, Semester 2 runs from February 2, 2026 to the June 2026 exam period and moves through Inquiry, Chemistry, Space, Electricity, and Biology/Ecology.

currentExamRange.subject: Chemistry
currentExamRange.note: Content posted on or after March 23, 2026 is part of the next Chemistry test range.

units.name: Inquiry
units.notes: Scientific questioning, controlled experiments, metric system, graphing, lab reports, and measurement investigations.

units.name: Chemistry
units.notes: Classification of matter, particle theory, physical and chemical change, periodic table, metals and non-metals, gas tests, atomic structure, bonding, and chemical formulas.

units.name: Space
units.notes: Earth-Moon-Sun interactions, eclipses, northern lights, spectroscopy, star life cycles, big bang theory, and space exploration.

units.name: Electricity
units.notes: Static electricity, charging, circuit diagrams, series and parallel circuits, current, voltage, resistance, and Ohm's law.

units.name: Biology / Ecology
units.notes: Energy flow, cycling of matter, biodiversity, climate and food security, and human effects on ecosystems.

keyDates.label: Inquiry unit test and rotocopter lab report due
keyDates.note: Inquiry assessment checkpoint before Chemistry begins.

keyDates.label: Chemistry quiz
keyDates.note: Includes learning up to and including physical and chemical change.

keyDates.label: Metals and non-metals lab analysis due in class
keyDates.note: Comes right before gas test prep and atomic theory work.

keyDates.label: Chemistry unit test
keyDates.note: Scheduled just before the Space unit starts on April 14, 2026.

keyDates.label: Space unit test
keyDates.note: Wraps the Space unit and begins the Electricity unit the same day.

keyDates.label: Electricity quiz: static electricity
keyDates.note: Mid-unit checkpoint during current electricity and circuits.

keyDates.label: Electricity unit test
keyDates.note: Final electricity assessment before Biology / Ecology starts.

keyDates.label: Ecology unit test
keyDates.note: Final unit test before review days and exam week.

keyDates.label: Exam period
keyDates.note: Block exam days begin June 18, 2026 and continue through June 24, 2026.

upcomingSupport.summary: Before class, learn how simple lab tests help identify common gases such as oxygen, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide.
beforeClass: Know that different gases can be identified with different test results.
Be ready to connect each gas to a specific observation in the lab.
Preview the safety idea that tests are done carefully with small samples.
Be ready to record detailed observations such as ignition, glowing splint result, colour, and visible changes.
quickQuestions: Which gas relights a glowing splint?
Which gas makes a small squeaky pop with a burning splint?
Which gas in this lab is linked to fire extinguishers?
Which gas helps support breathing in hospitals?
How can you connect a gas property to a real-life use?

resources.label: YouTube: Physics Teacher - Do you know your Gas Tests from Grade 9 science?

labSteps: Hydrogen: hydrochloric acid + magnesium ribbon, then test with a burning splint.
Oxygen: hydrogen peroxide + potassium iodide, then test with a glowing splint.
Carbon dioxide: vinegar + baking soda, then test with a burning splint and, if needed, a glowing splint.
propertyUseLinks: Hydrogen is flammable, so it can be used as a fuel source such as rocket fuel or other energy applications.
Oxygen supports combustion, so it is useful for medical breathing support and processes that need burning.
Carbon dioxide does not support combustion, so it is used in fire extinguishers.
teacherQuestions: How are the physical properties of the gases similar?
How are the physical properties different?
How are the chemical properties different?
Which gas is used in fire extinguishers and why?
Which gas was used in balloons and why is it no longer commonly used?
Which gas is used to help hospital patients breathe?
Pick one property and explain why it makes that substance useful in real life.
Explain why a material's physical property can make it a good choice for a job, like carbon in pencil lead.
flexibleThinkingExamples: Non-metal carbon in pencil lead is soft enough to rub onto paper, so that physical property makes it useful for writing.
A teacher may ask for a property-to-use explanation, not just the name of the gas or substance.
Practice answering in this format: property -> what it means -> why that helps in the real-world use.

topics.summary: The current Chemistry test range starts with the March 23, 2026 content and continues through the most recent posted class materials.
keyConcepts: Coding for Science connections where assigned
teacherNotes: March 23, 2026 and later Chemistry posts are part of the next test range.
Use recent Google Classroom materials as the main review source.
feynmanChecklist: I can explain the main periodic table patterns.
I can describe the key idea of atomic theory in simple words.
